commit.message:
style: minor formatting tweaks across section components

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix relationship invite: create group so relationships show in panel

The acceptInvite flow set inseparable_with_id but never created a Group
or set group_id. The person detail panel reads relationships from
group.members, so invited relationships were invisible in the UI.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(ui): correct custom themes path, reactive preset options, profile layout

Change custom themes directory from /var/lib/meridian-dns/custom_themes
to /var/meridian-dns/custom_themes in ThemeRoutes.hpp and CUSTOM_THEMES.md.
Make darkPresets/lightPresets arrays reactive so custom themes registered
via loadCustomPresets() appear in profile dropdown selects. Redesign
ProfileView as a two-column CSS grid (Profile+Password left, Appearance
right, API Keys full-width) for better horizontal space usage at 1280x800+
with single-column fallback at <=860px.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add cylinder contribution test, deep diagnostic probe, and recording data

New --cylinder-test command: disables each injector via STEUERN_EV_x and
measures RPM drop for power balance testing. Updated analysis thresholds
for lambda integrators (2.5% stdev = normal closed-loop) and O2 sensor
switching rate (Nyquist-aware with distribution analysis fallback).

Key diagnostic findings from this session:
- O2 heater duty asymmetry: B1=10% vs B2=59%
- IAT sensor reading 64.5C flat (likely substitute value, no real sensor)
- Cold start snapshot: IAT=141C, RPM@N+10=0, RPM@N+40=3200
- Bank 1 roughness consistently 5x bank 2 (cyl 2-3-4 worst)
- Plugs and coils are new — misfires are not ignition-side
- Suspected incomplete Dinan supercharger removal (IAT wiring, vacuum)

Includes two recording CSVs: 4-min session with throttle adjustments
and 1-min post-tape baseline at 936rpm.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add project-level resource management (#161)

## Summary
- Add a **Resources tab** to ProjectDetail for managing project-scoped
resources (`<project_dir>/.strawpot/`)
- New backend router with 8 endpoints (list, detail, install, uninstall,
update, reinstall, get/save config) using `strawhub --root <dir>`
instead of `--global`
- Existing components (ResourceDetailSheet, ResourceConfigForm,
InstallDialog) now accept optional `projectId` to switch between global
and project scope
- Fix `-y` flag placement in strawhub CLI calls — must follow subcommand
name, not precede it

## Test plan
- [ ] Navigate to a project, verify Resources tab appears alongside
Sessions and Files
- [ ] Install a resource to the project — verify it appears in the table
and lands in `<project_dir>/.strawpot/`
- [ ] Click resource row — detail sheet opens with config form,
update/reinstall/uninstall buttons
- [ ] Uninstall a project resource — verify removal
- [ ] Global resource management still works unchanged
- [ ] Reinstall no longer shows "Aborted" error (fixed `-y` flag
position)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
bd-d8f4: Fleet Sync MCP tool restoration with explicit tool classes (#332)

* feat(dx-fleet): MCP fail-closed semantics + strict freshness (bd-d8f4 P0/P1)

Implements Fleet Sync V2.2 prod-readiness P0/P1 items:

- P0: MCP overall truth model
  * Compute overall from BOTH tool rows and file rows
  * Add summary fields: tools_pass/tools_warn/tools_fail, files_pass/files_warn/files_fail
  * If any tool fail => overall=red

- P0: Runtime error fail-closed
  * Remove stale cached JSON fallback
  * Emit synthetic red JSON on runtime errors
  * Exit non-zero after emitting JSON
  * Include deterministic error details and next_action

- P0: Fleet check strict MCP interpretation + freshness
  * Fail on tools_fail>0 even if .overall=green
  * Enforce freshness using generated_at_epoch
  * Emit reason codes: local_snapshot_stale, remote_snapshot_stale, remote_snapshot_missing
  * Check remote MCP tool health strictly

- P1: Fleet-wide converge command
  * dx-fleet converge [--apply|--check|--repair]
  * Returns non-zero if any host red
  * Includes host-level summary + reason codes

Feature-Key: bd-d8f4
Agent: opencode

* docs(fleet-sync): add V2.2 fail-closed semantics + operator runbook (bd-d8f4)

Updates to FLEET_SYNC_SPEC.md and FLEET_SYNC_RUNBOOK.md:

- Document MCP overall truth model (tools + files)
- Runtime error fail-closed behavior
- Strict freshness enforcement semantics
- Fleet-wide converge command usage
- Operator one-command converge + repair loop
- Updated command examples with new converge subcommand

This prevents false-green fleet health reports.

## Changes

### Architecture
- Add explicit `integration_mode` field to mcp-tools.yaml schema
- Support two tool classes: `mcp` (IDE-rendered) and `cli` (standalone)
- Only MCP tools are rendered into IDE configs
- CLI tools only require Layer 1 host runtime health

### Tool Updates
- llm-tldr: Confirmed working (integration_mode: mcp)
- cass-memory: Fixed install, confirmed working (integration_mode: cli)
- context-plus: Fixed package name (contextplus@1.0.7), confirmed working
- serena: Documented but disabled pending validation

### Skills
- NEW: extended/fleet-sync/SKILL.md - Fleet Sync orchestrator skill
- NEW: extended/serena/SKILL.md - Serena skill (disabled)
- UPDATED: extended/cass-memory/SKILL.md - CLI-native documentation
- UPDATED: extended/context-plus/SKILL.md - Correct install commands
- UPDATED: extended/llm-tldr/SKILL.md - MCP documentation

### Scripts
- dx-mcp-tools-sync.sh: Handle cli vs mcp tool classes
- Only render MCP tools to IDE configs

### Docs
- FLEET_SYNC_SPEC.md: Added Tool Classes section, updated status
- FLEET_SYNC_RUNBOOK.md: Updated tool roster and operator expectations

## Validation
- Layer 1: All tools passing health checks
- Layer 2: Config convergence green
- Layer 3: Fleet daily check green (4/4 hosts)
- Layer 4: MCP tools visible in IDE configs

repository.name: mtg-meta-analyzer
repository.description: Tournament metagame analysis for competitive Magic: The Gathering.

commit.message:
Improve puzzle quality with embedding feedback loop and category history

Three generator improvements based on validation run analysis:

- Fix category_type drop in _step5_assemble — was silently defaulting all
  groups to members_of_set, causing the embedding validator to apply
  coherence checks to fill_in_the_blank and wordplay groups incorrectly

- Add Step 3.5 embedding bridge analysis — runs the embedding validator
  between group building and refinement, surfaces bridge words (words that
  sit between groups in semantic space) to Step 4 so swaps are targeted
  at the words most likely to cause solver confusion

- Add dynamic category history injection — queries puzzle_groups in
  Supabase for recently generated category names and injects them into the
  Step 2 brainstorm prompt to prevent the generator repeating themes like
  "Slang for money" across successive runs; degrades gracefully if
  Supabase is unavailable or created_at column is missing

Also lowers PASSING_SCORE_THRESHOLD from 0.6 to 0.5 based on 19 runs of
calibration data.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Handle tournaments with standings-less final rounds

Some tournaments (e.g. RC finals) have a final playoff round that
contains match results but no standings endpoint. The fetch script now
walks backwards from the last completed round to find standings, then
uses applyStandingsLessRoundAdjustments() to infer final rankings from
match outcomes. Also re-fetches 392336 with no-show cleaning applied.

commit.message:
CAS sharpening on Adreno GPU; WinML NPU session via 1x1 Conv

CAS sharpening pipeline (Adreno GPU): working end-to-end.

WinML NPU pipeline: depthwise group Conv (3x3, group=3) rejected by all
WinML backends with ERROR_FILE_CORRUPT during session compilation.
Switched to 1x1 standard Conv (group=1, weight [3,3,1,1]) which creates
a valid WinML session on DirectXMinPower (Hexagon NPU on Snapdragon X).

Current NPU model is an identity passthrough (3x3 identity matrix weights).
This establishes NPU pipeline; sharpening weights to be added in follow-up.

ONNX encoding fixes: ir_version=7, packed repeated int64 for attributes,
concrete static input shape [1,3,1440,2560], model file kept on disk.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix D'-apostrophe polygon matching + guard logic improvements

- D'-map: when polygon uses D' contraction (e.g. "PASSO D'AREIA"), build a
  lookup from the DA-expanded form ("PASSO DA AREIA") → original polygon name.
  Handles the case where D' expansion at top of function produces "PASSO DA AREIA"
  but the polygon stores "PASSO D'AREIA". Applies wherever OSM uses D' in polygon
  name_normalized — fully general.
- Fix guard: old condition "if result != bairro_norm: pass" prevented further
  matching after ANY transformation (D' expansion, abbreviation, prefix expansion).
  New condition requires the transformation to have actually found a polygon match.
  Enables cases like "STA CECILIA" → "SANTA CECILIA" (prefix expansion) →
  "CECILIA" (reverse-suffix) to work end-to-end.
- All inner guards changed from result==bairro_norm to result not in poly_names,
  which is semantically correct and consistent with each step's purpose.

commit.message:
feat: PageIndex unification — shared tree for compliance, plugin builder, evaluation

Phase 1: Foundation
- Fix compliance-evaluate page_range bug: tree nodes use start_index/end_index,
  not page_range. Added _compact_tree() with recursive child sections so LLM
  sees actual page ranges for navigation.
- PageIndex Lambda: supports entityType/entityId for storing trees on baselines
  (referenceTree map) and plugins (sampleTree map). documentId now optional
  for standalone reference doc processing.
- New API endpoint POST /documents/build-tree: async PageIndex invocation for
  any document (reference docs, plugin samples, regular documents).
- CDK: PageIndex Lambda gets baselines + plugin table grants and env vars.
  API Lambda gets PageIndex invoke grant.

Phase 2: Compliance Policy Builder
- Compliance-ingest: tree-assisted extraction — when PageIndex tree exists for
  a reference PDF, iterates tree sections and runs Sonnet 4.6 per section
  instead of truncating raw text. Each requirement tagged with sourceSection
  and sourcePages. Falls back to raw text for non-PDF or missing trees.
- BaselineEditor: after uploading PDFs, triggers PageIndex tree build, polls
  until tree_ready, then triggers requirement extraction.

Phase 3: Plugin Builder
- PluginWizard: after analyzing each sample PDF, fires background PageIndex
  tree build (fire-and-forget). Tree available for future AI refinement.

Phase 4: Polish
- Compliance-evaluate compact tree includes nested sections for better navigation

fix: replace Stooq with Yahoo Finance to resolve CORS errors

- Switch all market data to query1.finance.yahoo.com/v8/finance/quote
- Update all symbols to Yahoo Finance format (^GSPC, ^N225, GC=F, etc.)
- Add automatic CORS-proxy fallback via corsproxy.io if direct fetch fails
- Batch all 14 indices+commodities into a single Yahoo API call
- Use regularMarketPrice and regularMarketChangePercent (proper daily
  change vs previous close, more accurate than Stooq open/close math)
- Keep Frankfurter for forex (CORS-native, no proxy needed)
- BIST100 now shows USD equivalent in sub-line (price is native TRY)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Report generation (sales, stock, invoice_summary) with markdown + Chart.js
- Dynamic form creation with 5 themes, 10 field types, hosted on S3
- Form submission (public) stores in DynamoDB + Pinecone indexing
- Form submissions retrieval with pagination
- MCP server: report_generate, report_list, report_get, form_create,
  form_list, form_get, form_submissions tools
- Migrations 005 (reports/forms columns) and 006 (submission_count)
- DEPLOYMENT.md updated with new routes and env vars

commit.message:
docs: fix boot sequence step ordering to match implementation

RNDR check happens before heap init in both the code and expected
output. Split step 8/9 to reflect actual order: timer → RNDR check →
heap → identity → idle loop.

commit.message:
Initial release v0.2.0 — rate limit auto-resume + context exhaustion handling

Auto-resume Claude Code sessions on rate limits with smart wait time parsing.
Three-layer context window monitoring: status line (live %), PreCompact hook
(logging + notifications), SessionStart hook (task state recovery via
additionalContext injection after compaction).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
